Off Calendar Mar-05-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Byron Done for and with Bruno Reepen, Ronald Reepen and Mary Baird (Petitioners/Administrators).

Mr. Done stated that the petitioners have seen the schedules. Bruno, Ronald and Mary all indicated that they are familiar with the schedules and verified under penalty of perjury that the information is true. The Petition for Final Distribution upon Waiver of Account, etc. is under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Aug-02-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Byron Done for Petitioners

Mr. Done stated that he has filed a waiver of notices and accountings by remaining heir(s)/beneficiaries, he has received a withdrawal for request for special notice by the attorney for the heir, and he is ready to file the petition for final distribution. The Court granted The Petition for Approval of Status of Administration (subject to review of the order). Status on filing a Petition for Final Distribution or Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration is set for September 5, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Jun-27-2018 Continued To Jul-25-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Byron Done for Administrators.

The Court informed Mr. Done that he needs to re-file the proof of service, and that he needs to use a notice of hearing on the Judicial Council form because the required language needs to be in the notice. The Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration is continued to July 25, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Off Calendar May-08-2018 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-13-2018 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Byron Done for Petitioners

Mr. Done stated they were waiting for opinions from accountant regarding the taxes to be reviewed and his clients wanted to get a second opinion on how to minimize tax consequences. The Court informed Counsel that it is inclined to take this petition off calendar for filing of a proper petition. The Final Report on Waiver of Account, etc. is Off Calendar. Status for filing of petition for Final Distribution or Petition for Approval on Status Report to Continue Administration is set for May 8, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Counsel is to advise his client that something needs to get done or the court will consider suspending the Administrators.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le, Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-27-2018 Continued To Mar-13-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-09-2018 Continued To Feb-27-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Byron Done for Petitioners.

Mr. Done requested a 60-day continuance stating that parties have agreed to distribution under the terms of the trust, that one of the beneficiaries began the process of getting an opinion on the tax implications, that he got something from the accountant, but he was unable to review it, and that they are still waiting on that issue. The Final Report on Waiver of Account, etc. is continued to February 27, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-14-2017 Continued To Jan-09-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Byron Done for Mary Baird.

Mr. Done stated that they were able to reach an agreement between the Beneficiaries of the Decedent and the Trust, that they are working on it, that they are getting assignments done in both Matilda's Trust and George's Estate, and asked to come back in late December or early January to have a final petition heard. The Final Report on Waiver of Account, etc. is continued to January 9, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the expectation that an amended petition will be filed by then and noticed.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-17-2017 Continued To Nov-14-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-19-2017 Continued To Oct-17-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Byron Done for Co-Administrators.

Mr. Done stated that he was told he needed to wrap up the Trust before moving to close the Estate, that the Trustee is working very hard to do that, that the Trustee provided an eleven year accounting and that she has tried to resolve distribution of the property. Mr. Done also stated that the Trustee valued the properties, that one of the Beneficiaries is insisting on a market sale, that she is working with agents and looking into listing agreements, that he hopes to report sale soon and that there is another Estate involved for one of the siblings. The Court made Counsel aware that it can reduce fees if delay is the result of the administrator. The Final Report on Waiver of Account, etc. is continued to October 17, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-08-2017 Continued To Sep-19-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-13-2017 Continued To Jul-18-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-10-2017 Continued To Jun-13-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Byron Done for and with Mary Baird, Ronald Reepen and Bruno Reepen (C0-Administrators).

Mr. Done stated that the Trust is composed of four properties that distributed the one fifth interest to the Estate, that Daniel's (Beneficiary) interest is in probate and that the Decedent's interest was distributed to the Estate a month and a half ago. Mr. Done also stated that there is a checking account with probably less than 1% of the corpus, that it can be dealt with in the order as an after acquired asset, that it is an operating account for rents and expenses, and that he anticipates the Trust closing over the summer. Ms. Baird indicated that checking account has $65,000.00 in it. The Court informed Counsel that the right way to do it is to close out the Trust, and then close the Estate when it receives what it is owed. The Final Report on Waiver of Account; Petition for Fees and Petition for Final Distribution is continued to June 13, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to amend the petition to address this issue.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-26-2017 Continued To May-10-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-21-2017 Continued To Apr-26-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-14-2017 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-14-2017 Continued To Mar-21-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Payette for and with petitioners Mary Baird, Ron Reepen and Bruno Reepen; Robert Peterson for Karen Tello-Reepen as Administrator of the Estate of Daniel Reepen.

Mr. Payette reported that he had submitted the petiton for e-filing prior to the prior hearing date of January 10, 2017, but that the submittal had been rejected by the Court's e-filing clerk. He stated that he had been under the impression that he did not need to appear on January 10 if the petition was filed, and he was unaware that the filing was rejected until later. This explained why he did not appear on January 10th. The Court replied that Mr. Payette had still not corrected the deficiency in the filing and resubmitted the petition for final distribution, and that her was concerned about counsel's failure to act diligently in bringing the matter to resolution. He further stated that the parties have been discussing a resolution of the matter, and that such would include a waiver of an accounting. Mr. Peterson acknowledged that a settlement offer had been presented which his client was considering, but that his client has not agreed to a waiver of an accounting. He added that the real problem in this matter was that the real property was part of the Matilda Reepen Trust which has never been distributed, and that the George Reepen Estate lacked valid title which would allow the property to be distributed at this time. He wondered why the petitioners have failed to resolve matters and get their petition for final distribution on file. The Court expressed his concern over the ongoing failure of the current administrators to get the petition on file which was purportedly prepared over a month ago. The matter was continued to March 14, 2017 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy A. Jaime. (Not reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-10-2017 Continued To Feb-14-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Robert Peterson for Karen Reepen (Beneficiary of the Estate of Daniel Reepen).

The Court noted that Mr. Payette was not present and that nothing has been filed. Mr. Peterson stated that he believes the Administrator should be removed, that assets have been distributed from Matilda's Trust, that they can not distribute George's Estate without receiving distribution and asked that a Professional Fiduciary be appointed. An Order to Appear will be issued to the Attorney and his Clients to appear on February 14,2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and show why they should not be removed as Administrators. If the Petition for Final Distribution is not filed by that date, the Court will consider removal. If the petition is filed no later than January 20, 2017, the Court will allow it to be heard on February 14, 2017. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-20-2016 Continued To Jan-10-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Payette for and with (co-administrator) Mary Baird.

Mr. Payette reported that he has the petition for final distribution, that it will be e-filed today, that there is no waiver of accounting and that all of the administrators have signed it. The accounting petition was continued to January 10, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the expectation that it will come off calendar as the petition will have been filed.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Nov-22-2016 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-22-2016 Continued To Dec-20-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Payette for and with Mary Baird and Ronald Reepen.

Mr. Payette reported that Bruno Reepen was on his way but not here yet, explained that their non appearance on October 25, 2016 was due to a calendaring error and that they actually appeared on October 26, 2016. The Court stated that it has now been another 30 days and things are still not done, that they have not filed the proper documents, that the Inventory & Appraisal was only signed by one administrator and that nothing has been filed to close the estate. Mr. Payette stated that there have been 2 declarations filed and signed by all 3 administrators, that they have finally appraised the two cars which had been taken by Daniel Reepen one of which was donated and the other sold for $10.00 and that the attorney for Daniel Reepen's estate has been notified. The Court asked when this estate will close. Mr. Payette stated that they have asked the attorney for Daniel Reepen's estate to waive the accounting, that they will file a corrected Inventory & Appraisal, that there are two properties in which Daniel's estate is entitled to 25%, that they have had the properties appraised and are in the process of obtaining a loan to pay Daniel's share to his estate. The Court stated that the current pending petition cannot go forward and that by December 20, 2016 it expects that either a new petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of a status report be filed. The Petition for final distribution was continued to December 20, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court expressed its concern that things are not getting done, that any petition that is filed must be joined in by all 3 administrators and that the final Inventory & Appraisal but be signed by all 3 administrators.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Yolanda Mazariegos. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-25-2016 Continued To Nov-22-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court stated that this matter is on for failure to respond to the examiner's notes, that this is not the first time that counsel has not appeared and an Order to Appear has to be issued. An Order to Appear will be issued to the Co-administrators and the Attorney Steven Payette to appear on November 22, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why the various items have not been filed. The status hearing is also continued to that date. The Court will consider suspension at the hearing if nothing has been done to address the issues.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-27-2016 Continued To Oct-25-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Payette for and with (trustee) Mary Baird.

The Court stated that the procedural issues still have not been addressed. Mr. Payette reported that the accounting has been prepared, that they are still waiting on the appraisal from the Probate Referee, that in regards to Daniel Reepen's estate they have a copy of the letters and will file them with the accounting and that Daniel's estate is being probated in San Francisco with his wife as administrator. The Court asked if all of the administrator's will be signing the accounting. Mr. Payette responded yes, that he intends to meet with the probate referee to get the appraisal and will get it filed and then they will be able to complete the accounting. The Court asked if there is any way to say when it will be done and Mr. Payette responded that it will be filed next week. The accounting petition was continued to October 25, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-31-2016 Continued To Sep-27-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Payette for (co-administrators) Ronald, Bruno & Daniel Reepen and Mary Baird who is present.

The Court stated that there was a discussion at the last hearing and that nothing has been done. Mr. Payette stated that they did not finish the accounting. The Court stated that with the death of the co-personal representative something needs to be done regarding the status of the others and that what is in front of the court is not property verified. Mr. Payette stated that he needs two weeks to get everything done, that an estate has been opened for Daniel Reepen in South San Francisco and that his spouse has been named as executrix. The Court continued the accounting petition to September 27, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and stated that it expects that everything will come in and that it will address the issues raised.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Aug-03-2016 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Payette for and with (administrator) Mary Baird. Britta Peterson for (beneficiary) Karen Tello-Reepen.

Mr. Payette reported that he received the notes, that he discussed the issues with the administrator, that she then left for vacation and is just now back, that he himself will be leaving for vacation and that they would like a continuance to put together the accounting. The Court stated that they are here under an Order to Appear as they failed to appear at the last hearing. Mr. Payette stated that he was ill. Ms. Peterson stated that she is here to observe, that they do not know what the holdup is, that the matter has been continued 6 times, that there is a letter stating that the continuances are because Mr. Payette was ill but that it also pointed a finger at them. The Court stated that there are procedural complications regarding the death of Mr. Reepen and that it may expand the check list of what needs to be done. The Petition for final distribution was continued to August 31, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and the Order to Appear is vacated.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)
